rulings.Case Title: RICHARD B FOX VS. BLUE SHIELD OF CALIFORNIA LIFE & HEALTH et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, APRIL 7, 2008 LINE 5. DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PHYSICIANS' SERVICE'S (D.B.A. BLUE SHIELD, BLUE SHIELD OF CALIFORNIA LIFE & HEALTH) DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF IS UNABLE TO PLEAD THE REQUISITE KNOWLEDGE. CASEY V U.S. BANK NAT'L. ASSOCIATION (2OO5) 127 CAL APP 4TH 1138, 1144. PLAINTIFF'S UNFAIR COMPETITION IS BARRED BY APPLICABLE CASE LAW. LORI RUBINSTEIN PHYSICAL THERAPY V P.T.P.N., INC. (2007) 148 CAL APP 4TH 1130, 1142; CEL-TECH COMMUNICATIONS, INC. V LOS ANGELES CELLULAR TELEPHONE CO. (1999) 20 CAL 4TH 163, 182. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: CLAUDIA GIOSEFFI VS. BENEFIT COSMETICS, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, APRIL 7, 2008 LINE 12. DEFENDANT BENEFIT COSMETICS, L.L.C.'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND IN TEN DAYS AS TO FIRST AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION. IF PLAINTIFF SEEKS PUNITIVE DAMAGES, PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE FACTS THAT SUPPORT THE CLAIM. AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF MUST DECIDE WHETHER PLAINTIFF SEEKS RELIEF UNDER 17200 OR 17500 AND PLEAD FACTS THAT SUPPORT THE CLAIM. SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION. AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF CONSENTED TO BEING TOUCHED AND HENCE, DOES NOT HAVE A CLAIM FOR BATTERY. =(302/PJM/VC)
